schema.description: A persistent knowledge-graph memory service that lets you create, relate, and query entities and their observations.

tools.name: create_entities
tools.description: Insert multiple entities into the knowledge graph, each with a name, type, and list of observations.

name.description: The unique name of the entity to be created.

entityType.description: The category or class of the entity (e.g., Person, Location, Event).

observations.description: List of factual statements or notes attached to this entity.

entities.description: Entity objects to insert into the graph.

name.description: Name of the successfully created entity.

skipped.description: Names of entities that already existed and were skipped.

tools.name: create_relations
tools.description: Create directed relationships between existing entities in the knowledge graph.

from.description: Name of the source entity where the relation starts.

to.description: Name of the target entity where the relation ends.

relationType.description: Label describing the nature of the relation (e.g., worksFor, locatedIn).

relations.description: Relation objects to insert into the graph.

from.description: Source entity name of the created relation.

to.description: Target entity name of the created relation.

skipped.description: Relations that could not be created (e.g., missing entities).

tools.name: add_observations
tools.description: Append new observations to existing entities in the knowledge graph.

entityName.description: Name of the entity to which observations will be added.

contents.description: List of observation strings to append.

observations.description: Observation objects specifying target entity and new facts.

contents.description: Observation strings that were successfully added.

added.description: Records confirming which observations were appended.

tools.name: search_nodes
tools.description: Search the knowledge graph for entities and relations matching a text query.

query.description: Text to match against entity names, types, and observation contents.

entities.description: Entities whose name, type, or observations contain the query text.

tools.name: open_nodes
tools.description: Retrieve full details of specific entities and any relations between them.

names.description: List of entity names to fetch.

entities.description: Detailed objects for each requested entity.

relations.description: Relations that exist among the requested entities.

scenarios.description: Single entity with no relations - tests basic entity creation and retrieval
Multiple entities with relations - tests a small social/org graph with bidirectional lookups
Rich knowledge graph with multiple entity types and diverse relations - tests traversal, type filtering, and observation querying

Alice.name: Alice
observations: Alice holds a PhD in Computer Science

ProjectAlpha.name: ProjectAlpha
observations: ProjectAlpha is a machine learning initiative

scenarios.description: Empty graph - tests tool behavior with no entities or relations

errors.details: Tool reports 'Entity 'Alice' not found' but entity was loaded in scenario and open_nodes can find it
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Add observation to existing Alice entity. Actual: Tool failed claiming Alice doesn't exist
errors.root_cause: The api.add_observations() method likely checks for entity existence using a different key than how entities are stored (e.g., case sensitivity issue, or entities are stored in api.scenario['entities'] but checked in api.entities)

errors.details: Search for 'Alice' returned empty entities list despite Alice being in the graph
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Return Alice entity in search results. Actual: Empty entities list
errors.root_cause: The api.search_nodes() method is not finding entities that were loaded via load_scenario. Entities may be stored in wrong data structure or search is looking in wrong location

errors.details: Alice's observations were corrupted - each character of the string 'Alice has 5 years of experience' became a separate observation. Also 'Bob' entity disappeared and 'Carol' appeared unexpectedly
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Alice's observations: ['Alice is a software engineer', 'Alice works at TechCorp', 'Alice has 5 years of experience']. Actual: Corrupted character array and 'Duplicate test' prefix
errors.root_cause: The add_observations method appears to be iterating over the string contents instead of treating it as an array. The 'contents' field expects a list of strings but received a string, and the code iterated over each character. Additionally, there appears to be cross-contamination between scenario states or the API is not properly isolated per client_id

errors.details: Saved scenario contains 'Carol' entity which was never created in this test, and is missing 'Bob' entity that was successfully created
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Entities Alice and Bob with correct observations. Actual: Entities Alice (corrupted) and Carol (never created)
errors.root_cause: The MemoryKnowledgeGraphAPI instance appears to be shared across all client sessions (singleton pattern), causing state contamination between different test runs. The API should be isolated per client_id

tool_execution.error: Entity 'Alice' not found
Search returned empty entities when Alice should exist
Observations corrupted - string was split into individual characters

save_scenario.error: Saved scenario does not match original: missing Bob, has Carol, and Alice's observations are corrupted

errors.details: 'str' object has no attribute 'get'
errors.root_cause: 'str' object has no attribute 'get'

errors.details: Scenario uses 'from_entity' and 'to_entity' fields, but create_relations tool expects 'from' and 'to' fields. Relations were likely not properly loaded.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected relations to be loaded with 'from'/'to' keys, but scenario provided 'from_entity'/'to_entity'. Tool validation checks for 'from'/'to' fields.
errors.root_cause: Inconsistent field naming between scenario schema and tool implementation. The load_scenario accepts the scenario as-is but create_relations tool validates for different field names.

errors.details: Alice entity observations corrupted - string "Alice has 5 years of experience" was split into individual characters: ['A', 'l', 'i', 'c', 'e', ' ', 'h', 'a', 's', ...]. Also shows 'Duplicate test' observation from test case.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: ["Alice is a software engineer", "Alice holds a PhD in Computer Science", "Alice is 34 years old", "Alice won the Best Engineer award in 2023"]. Actual: Corrupted array with character-level split strings.
errors.root_cause: Likely in add_observations or internal API handling - observations being processed as iterables character-by-character instead of as string elements.

errors.details: Search for 'software engineer' returned empty results despite Alice having this exact phrase in observations. Search for 'AI' returned empty despite TechCorp observation 'TechCorp specializes in AI products'.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected Alice entity for 'software engineer' query and TechCorp entity for 'AI' query. Actual: Empty results for both.
errors.root_cause: Search function may not be properly indexing or searching observation contents, or entities are not properly stored in the graph after loading.

errors.details: Request for ['Alice', 'Bob', 'TechCorp'] only returned Alice (with corrupted data). Request for ['ProjectAlpha', 'Python'] returned empty. All entities should exist from loaded scenario.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected all 6 original entities plus Charlie to be retrievable. Actual: Only Alice partially returned, others missing.
errors.root_cause: Entities may not be properly stored during load_scenario, or open_nodes has filtering/retrieval bug.

errors.details: After loading scenario and creating Charlie entity, save_scenario returned {"entities": {}, "relations": []} - completely empty state.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected scenario with all 6 original entities + Charlie, and all relations. Actual: Empty entities object and empty relations array.
errors.root_cause: Critical bug in either load_scenario not persisting data to API, or save_scenario not reading from correct API state. API instance may be isolated per call.

tool_execution.error: Tool silently failed to create valid relations. Relations between existing entities (Charlie -> TechCorp) were skipped without clear error reason
Search returning corrupted data. Entity observations split into individual characters. Searches for valid terms ("software engineer", "AI") return empty results
Returns incomplete data - only Alice entity returned when requesting multiple. Other requested entities missing. Observations data corrupted.

save_scenario.error: Scenario saved but returned empty entities and relations arrays. All loaded data was lost.

errors.details: When adding observation with contents 'Another observation', the tool returned contents as an array of individual characters: ["A","n","o","t","h","e","r"," ","o","b","s","e","r","v","a","t","i","o","n"] instead of a single string
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: {"added": [{"entityName": "TestEntity", "contents": ["Another observation"]}]}  |  Actual: {"added": [{"entityName": "TestEntity", "contents": ["A","n","o","t","h","e","r"," ","o","b","s","e","r","v","a","t","i","o","n"]}]}
errors.root_cause: The add_observations method in MemoryKnowledgeGraphAPI is likely iterating over the contents string and appending each character as a separate observation, or treating the string as an iterable instead of a single value. This is a bug in how the API handles the 'contents' field from the observation object.

tool_execution.error: add_observations returned incorrect output format - contents string was split into individual characters instead of being stored as a single observation string

errors.details: When add_observations is called with a single string in contents field, the string gets split into individual characters. For 'Alice has 5 years of experience', the result was ['h', '5', 'f', 'x', 'p'] instead of the full string.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: ['Alice has 5 years of experience'] | Actual: ['h', '5', 'f', 'x', 'p'] - individual characters from the string
errors.root_cause: The API's add_observations method appears to be iterating over the string contents instead of wrapping it in a list when a single string is passed. The tool wrapper does wrap strings in lists, but the API might be re-iterating or there's a character-level processing issue.

errors.details: After loading a clean scenario with only Alice entity, subsequent tool calls revealed pre-existing entities (Charlie, StartupXYZ) and relations (Alice-Charlie colleague_of, Charlie-TechCorp works_at, Alice-Bob collaborates_with) that were not part of the loaded scenario.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: State contains only Alice (and entities/relations we created during testing) | Actual: State contains Charlie, StartupXYZ, and pre-existing relations from unknown previous state
errors.root_cause: The MemoryKnowledgeGraphAPI instance may not be properly isolated per client_id. It appears to be a singleton or shared instance that retains data from previous test runs, violating the expected isolation for each client session.

errors.details: Alice entity shows observations that were not in the original loaded scenario: 'Duplicate Alice', 'Works remotely on Tuesdays', 'Active since 2023', plus the corrupted character-split observations.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: ['Alice is a software engineer', 'Alice works at TechCorp'] | Actual: Contains multiple unrelated observations plus corruption
errors.root_cause: This is a manifestation of the state pollution issue - the Alice entity was already modified before our test run began, suggesting the API wasn't in a clean state when load_scenario was called.

tool_execution.error: Observation string corruption - single string observations get split into individual characters instead of being stored as a single observation string
Search returns corrupted Alice observations; also returns data from previous test runs (Charlie, StartupXYZ entities and their relations) indicating state pollution
Returns corrupted Alice observations with split characters and polluted state from previous runs

save_scenario.error: Saved scenario contains pre-existing entities (Charlie, StartupXYZ) and corrupted Alice observations not in original scenario

errors.details: When passing a string value for 'contents' field, the tool splits the string into individual characters instead of treating it as a single observation. Input: 'Works remotely on Tuesdays', Output: ['W', 'o', 'r', 'k', 's', ...]
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: contents=['Works remotely on Tuesdays']. Actual: contents=['W', 'o', 'r', 'k', 's', ' ', 'r', 'e', 'm', 'o', 't', 'e', 'l', 'y', ' ', 'o', 'n', ' ', 'T', 'u', 'e', 's', 'd', 'a', 'y', 's']
errors.root_cause: The add_observations wrapper normalizes string to list but may be iterating over the string again at the API level, or there's a missing check. The code sets obs['contents'] = [contents] for string input, but somewhere the string is being treated as an iterable and split.

errors.details: Entity 'Alice' was loaded with observations ['Alice is a software engineer', 'Alice lives in New York']. When create_entities was called with 'Alice' having observation 'Duplicate Alice', the original observations were replaced instead of skipped. The saved scenario shows Alice has observations ['Duplicate Alice', ...split chars...] instead of preserving original data.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Alice should have been skipped or merged with existing. Original observations should be preserved. Actual: Alice observations were replaced entirely.
errors.root_cause: create_entities is not properly checking for existing entities before creating. The API allows entities with duplicate names to overwrite existing ones instead of being skipped.

errors.details: Original scenario loaded TechCorp as entityType='Organization' with observations ['TechCorp is a software company', 'Founded in 2010']. Search and save show TechCorp as entityType='Company' with observation 'TechCorp is a tech startup'.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: TechCorp entityType='Organization', observations=['TechCorp is a software company', 'Founded in 2010']. Actual: entityType='Company', observations=['TechCorp is a tech startup']
errors.root_cause: The scenario data loaded is not being stored correctly, or there's pre-existing data in the API that wasn't cleared, or the load_scenario implementation is not properly replacing existing data.

errors.details: Query 'software' returned empty entities array despite Alice having 'software engineer' and TechCorp having 'software company' in observations. Query 'New York' returned empty despite Alice 'lives in New York'.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: search_nodes('software') should return Alice and TechCorp entities. Actual: Empty results for both queries.
errors.root_cause: The search implementation may not be searching observation contents, or the stored data is different from what was loaded, or case sensitivity issues.

errors.details: Entity 'StartupXYZ' appears in saved scenario but was never created during the validation session.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Only entities from loaded scenario (Alice, Bob, TechCorp) plus created entity (Charlie). Actual: StartupXYZ also present.
errors.root_cause: The API instance may have pre-existing data that wasn't cleared by load_scenario, indicating load_scenario doesn't properly reset the graph state.

errors.details: Original relation loaded was 'Alice colleague_of Bob'. open_nodes and save_scenario show 'Alice collaborates_with Bob'.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: relationType='colleague_of'. Actual: relationType='collaborates_with'
errors.root_cause: Either the relation was never stored correctly, or there's data corruption during retrieval, or pre-existing data with different relation types exists.

tool_execution.error: String content incorrectly split into individual characters. Expected single observation 'Works remotely on Tuesdays' but got array of 26 single-character strings.
Search queries for 'software' and 'New York' returned empty results despite matching content in entities. Returned entity 'TechCorp' has different entityType and observations than loaded scenario.
Returned inconsistent data: Alice shows character-split observation, relation type changed from 'colleague_of' to 'collaborates_with', TechCorp has entityType 'Company' instead of 'Organization'.

save_scenario.error: Saved state does not match loaded scenario plus modifications. Original entities Alice, TechCorp have different observations/entityType. Original Bob is missing expected relations. Extra entity 'StartupXYZ' present that was not created during validation.

errors.details: When adding single string observation 'Alice received an award in 2023', the result was split into individual characters: ['A', 'i', 'c', 'c', 'i', 'v', 'w', 'i', '2', '0', '2', '3']
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Single observation string 'Alice received an award in 2023'. Actual: Array of single characters
errors.root_cause: The add_observations tool has a bug in string handling. Looking at the code, it normalizes contents but may be passing the string incorrectly to the API, causing iteration over characters instead of treating as a single observation.

errors.details: After loading scenario with entities Alice, Bob, TechCorp (Organization), ProjectAlpha, Python, MIT - the open_nodes call returns different data. Alice has corrupted observations (fragmented characters, 'Duplicate Alice', etc.), TechCorp has entityType 'Company' instead of 'Organization', and ProjectAlpha is missing from results despite being in the query.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Original scenario entities with correct observations. Actual: Corrupted/altered entity data that doesn't match the loaded scenario
errors.root_cause: The scenario loading may not be properly initializing/clearing state, or the API may have pre-existing data that wasn't cleared. The loaded scenario appears to have been merged with or overwritten by other data.

errors.details: Searching for 'Python' and 'software' returned empty entities and relations arrays, even though Python entity exists in the scenario and Alice has observations containing 'software engineer'.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Python entity and related entities returned. Actual: Empty results
errors.root_cause: The search_nodes API implementation may not be properly searching through observations, or the scenario data was not properly loaded/indexed for searching.

errors.details: Saved scenario contains only 5 entities (Charlie, Alice, StartupXYZ, Bob, TechCorp) instead of original 6+ entities. ProjectAlpha, Python, MIT are missing. Alice's observations are severely corrupted with character fragments. TechCorp has wrong entityType.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Original scenario with 6 entities plus any newly created ones. Actual: Mixed/corrupted state with wrong entity types and fragmented observations
errors.root_cause: The API state is shared across scenarios and not properly isolated. The scenario_003 load did not create a clean state, resulting in data from previous operations being mixed in.

errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected entities: Alice, Bob, TechCorp, ProjectAlpha, Python, MIT. Actual entities: Carol, Alice, Charlie. Expected 8+ relations, got 1.

low.details: When adding observation 'Alice has 10 years of experience', the returned data shows contents as ['1', '0', 'f', 'x', 'p'] - the string was split into individual characters
low.root_cause: The add_observations method likely has a bug where it iterates over the string instead of treating it as a single observation, or there's confusion between string and list handling in the API implementation.

low.details: Entities Bob, TechCorp, ProjectAlpha, Python, MIT from the loaded scenario cannot be found. search_nodes('Python') returns empty. open_nodes(['Python', 'MIT']) returns empty.
low.root_cause: The load_scenario did not actually load the scenario data into the isolated state, or the state was contaminated by other concurrent operations. The API appears to be using shared state across client sessions.

low.details: Entity 'Bob' not found error when trying to add observations to Bob, despite Bob being in the original scenario data
low.root_cause: Same root cause as above - scenario data was not properly loaded/isolated, so Bob entity doesn't exist in the actual working state.

low.type: Tool logic error - String handling bug
low.details: When passing contents as a string (not a list), the string 'Lives in New York' was incorrectly split into individual characters ['L', 'i', 'v', 'e', 's', ' ', 'i', 'n', ' ', 'N', 'e', 'w', ' ', 'Y', 'o', 'r', 'k']
low.root_cause: The API implementation likely iterates over contents without checking if it's a string. In Python, iterating over a string yields individual characters. The code should check if contents is a string and wrap it in a list, or if it's already a list, use it directly.

error_patterns.type: Tool logic error - String handling bug

errors.details: Original Alice entity had observations ['Alice is a software engineer', 'Alice works at TechCorp'] in the loaded scenario, but these were not preserved. Search for 'software' returned empty.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Alice retains original observations. Actual: Alice has different observations ['Duplicate Alice'], suggesting original data was not properly stored.
errors.root_cause: The api.load_scenario() method may not be properly storing entity observations, or there is cross-contamination between API instances/sessions.

errors.details: Tool failed with 'Entity 'Alice' not found' but Alice is clearly visible in other tools (search_nodes, open_nodes).
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: add_observations should find Alice and add observations. Actual: Entity not found error despite Alice existing in the graph.
errors.root_cause: Different tools may be accessing different internal states of the API. The api.entities dictionary used by add_observations may not contain Alice, while search_nodes and open_nodes use a different data structure.

errors.details: Saved scenario contains entity 'Charlie' with observations ['Charlie is a new developer'] and a relation Alice->Charlie 'manages'. Alice's observations are corrupted into individual characters.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Entities Alice, Bob, TechCorp with their created observations. Actual: Entities Alice (corrupted), Charlie (uncreated), and unexpected relation.
errors.root_cause: Critical state management issue. The API appears to be sharing state across different sessions/client_ids, or the internal state is being corrupted. The string 'Alice is working on Project X' was split into individual characters as observations.

errors.details: Created TechCorp via create_entities (confirmed success), but open_nodes(names=['TechCorp']) returned empty entities list.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: TechCorp entity returned. Actual: Empty entities list.
errors.root_cause: The create_entities tool may not be properly persisting to the same data store that open_nodes reads from, or entities are being stored under different keys/names.

errors.details: All relations were skipped even though from/to entities (Alice, Bob, TechCorp) exist in the graph.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Relations created successfully. Actual: All relations skipped with empty created list.
errors.root_cause: The api.create_relations method may be checking for entity existence in a different data structure than where entities are stored, or the entity lookup is failing due to name/key mismatch.

tool_execution.error: Entity overwrite behavior: When creating Alice (which already existed), the tool overwrote her observations instead of skipping or merging. Original observations ['Alice is a software engineer', 'Alice works at TechCorp'] were replaced with ['Duplicate Alice']

All relations were skipped despite entities existing: Tried to create relations between Alice->TechCorp and Bob->TechCorp (both entities exist), but all were returned as skipped. Expected behavior unclear from documentation.

Entity 'Alice' not found - Critical bug: The tool raised an error saying 'Alice' was not found, but Alice definitely exists (confirmed by search_nodes and open_nodes). This suggests API state inconsistency between tools.
Lost original data: Search for 'software' returned empty even though scenario loaded Alice with 'Alice is a software engineer'. Original observations were lost during load or entity creation.
Missing entities: Requested [Alice, Bob] but only returned Alice. Requested [TechCorp] but returned empty. TechCorp was created by create_entities but is not retrievable.

save_scenario.error: State corruption detected: Saved scenario contains completely different data than expected. Contains entity 'Charlie' that was never created, corrupted observations for Alice (each character stored as separate observation), and a relation that was never created.

errors.details: Charlie was skipped when creating new entity, Alice was overwritten instead of being skipped when attempting to create duplicate
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Charlie created with 'Charlie is a data scientist'. Actual: Charlie skipped, Alice overwritten with 'Duplicate Alice'
errors.root_cause: The API's create_entities method appears to have incorrect logic for determining which entities exist. The loaded scenario entities may not be properly registered in the API's entity lookup.

errors.details: String observation was converted to character array. Input: 'Alice is working on Project X'. Output: ['A', 'l', 'i', 'c', 'e', ' ', 'i', 's', ...]
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Observation stored as string. Actual: Observation stored as array of individual characters
errors.root_cause: The add_observations method appears to be treating the string as an iterable and iterating over characters instead of treating it as a single observation. This suggests improper type checking or string handling.

errors.details: Search for 'engineer' returned no results even though Alice has observation 'Alice is a software engineer'
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Alice entity in search results. Actual: Empty entities array
errors.root_cause: Search algorithm may not be properly searching through observations, or the observations were not properly stored during scenario loading.

errors.details: Requested Alice and Bob, but only Alice was returned. Bob entity appears to be missing from the API state.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Both Alice and Bob entities. Actual: Only Alice entity returned
errors.root_cause: Entities loaded from scenario may not be properly persisted, or get lost during API operations. Only the newly created/updated Alice entity is accessible.

errors.details: Original scenario entities (Bob, TechCorp) missing from saved state. Charlie's observations changed. Original relations lost.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Original 3 entities + new Charlie with original observations and all relations. Actual: 2 entities (Alice, Charlie) with modified observations, only 1 relation.
errors.root_cause: The API is not properly maintaining state across operations. Scenario loading appears to work (returns success) but entities are not properly stored or are being lost during subsequent operations.

tool_execution.error: Expected behavior: create_entities should add new entity Charlie with provided observations. Actual: Charlie was skipped (added to 'skipped' list) even though not in original scenario. Also, when trying to create Alice (already exists), API allowed it and replaced original Alice instead of skipping it.
Expected: Should create relation between Alice and Charlie. Actual: Relation created but when querying entities via open_nodes, Charlie entity not found (only Alice exists in state).

Expected: Should append observation string to Alice. Actual: String 'Alice is working on Project X' was incorrectly converted to individual characters array ['A', 'l', 'i', 'c', 'e', ...]. This is a data handling bug in the API.
Expected: Should find Alice when searching for 'engineer' (observation: 'Alice is a software engineer'). Actual: Returned empty entities array. Search functionality is not working correctly.

Expected: Should return both Alice and Bob entities. Actual: Only returned Alice, Bob was missing from results. Also, Alice's observations contained incorrectly formatted data (character arrays instead of strings).

save_scenario.error: State inconsistency detected: Original scenario had 3 entities (Alice, Bob, TechCorp), but saved scenario only has 2 (Alice, Charlie). Original observations for Alice were modified/replaced. Bob and TechCorp entities disappeared. Charlie's observations were changed from 'Charlie is a data scientist' to 'Charlie is a new developer'. Original relations were not preserved.

errors.details: When passing a single string as 'contents' to add_observations (e.g., "New observation added"), the string is split into individual characters instead of being treated as a single observation.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: contents='New observation added' to be stored as a single observation string. Actual: Stored as ['N', 'e', 'w', ' ', 'o', 'b', 's', 'e', 'r', 'v', 'a', 't', 'i', 'o', 'n', ' ', 'a', 'd', 'd', 'e', 'd'] - an array of individual characters.
errors.root_cause: The API implementation appears to be iterating over string characters when it should check if contents is a string (not a list) and wrap it in a list, or treat it as a single element. Works correctly when contents is already a list of strings.

errors.details: Relations saved with 'from_entity' and 'to_entity' field names instead of 'from' and 'to' used in create_relations input.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: relations to use consistent field names 'from' and 'to' throughout. Actual: create_relations uses 'from'/'to', save_scenario returns 'from_entity'/'to_entity'.
errors.root_cause: Internal API uses different field names for input vs output serialization. This is a minor inconsistency that doesn't affect functionality but could cause confusion for consumers expecting consistent field names.

tool_execution.notes: Observation bug noted: single string observations are split into character arrays instead of being treated as a single observation

errors.details: String observation 'Alice is learning Python' was stored as array of individual characters: ["A", "l", "i", "c", "e", " ", "i", "s", " ", "l", "e", "a", "r", "n", "i", "n", "g", " ", "P", "y", "t", "h", "o", "n"]
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: contents as string 'Alice is learning Python'. Actual: contents as character array
errors.root_cause: The add_observations implementation likely treats the string as an iterable and iterates over characters, or the API incorrectly handles string vs list input for observations

errors.details: When attempting to create entity 'Alice' (which already existed from scenario load), the entity was overwritten instead of skipped. Original observations ('Alice is a software engineer', 'Alice works at TechCorp') were lost.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Alice entity to be skipped (empty created array). Actual: Alice was overwritten with new observation 'Duplicate Alice'
errors.root_cause: create_entities does not check for existing entities before creating - it should verify entity existence and return it in 'skipped' array without modifying existing data

errors.details: Saved scenario contains entities not created during this validation: Charlie (Person), Carol (Person), DataPlatform (Technology). These entities have observations indicating they were from another test scenario.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Only Alice, Bob entities and 'works_with' relation. Actual: Contains 5 entities total including unexpected ones
errors.root_cause: Either the MemoryKnowledgeGraphAPI instance is shared across different scenario validations (singleton pattern issue), or state is not being properly isolated/cleared between tests

save_scenario.error: Saved scenario contains unexpected entities (Charlie, Carol, DataPlatform) and missing original Alice observations

errors.details: Tool accepts relations with 'from' and 'to' keys, but the API likely expects 'from_entity' and 'to_entity' keys (as used in scenario data). Relation {from: 'Carol', to: 'TechCorp', relationType: 'works_at'} was skipped despite both entities existing.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: relation created successfully. Actual: relation skipped with no error explanation.
errors.root_cause: The tool wrapper uses different field names ('from'/'to') than what the underlying API expects ('from_entity'/'to_entity'), causing a mismatch when the API tries to look up entities.

errors.details: When 'contents' is a single string, the tool iterates over characters instead of treating it as a single observation. Input 'Alice won an award in 2023' was split into ['w', 'w', 'd', '2', '0', '2', '3'].
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: ['Alice won an award in 2023']. Actual: ['w', 'w', 'd', '2', '0', '2', '3']
errors.root_cause: The API likely expects 'contents' to be a list. When a string is passed, Python iterates over it character by character. The tool should wrap single strings in a list before calling the API.

errors.details: After loading scenario and creating new entities, save_scenario returned empty entities and relations. All data loaded via load_scenario was lost.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: 8 entities (6 original + Carol + DataPlatform) and 9 relations (8 original + Alice collaborates_with Bob). Actual: empty entities {}, empty relations []
errors.root_cause: The load_scenario method in the API likely doesn't properly persist data to the entities/relations stores that save_scenario reads from. The API might be using separate data structures or the load operation is not integrated with the entity/relation creation methods.

errors.details: search_nodes returned results for 'data' query (Charlie, DataPlatform) but not for 'MIT' or 'software' queries. open_nodes returns empty for all valid entity names. The search and retrieval logic appears broken or querying a different data store.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: search_nodes('software') should find Alice. search_nodes('MIT') should find Bob and MIT entity. open_nodes(['Alice', 'Bob']) should return both entities. Actual: empty results or inconsistent matches.
errors.root_cause: The load_scenario likely populated a different data structure than what search_nodes and open_nodes query. The API may have inconsistent internal state or the tools are querying different collections.

tool_execution.error: Schema mismatch: Valid relations between existing entities (Carol -> TechCorp) were incorrectly skipped. Tool expects 'from'/'to' keys but API expects 'from_entity'/'to_entity' keys.
String handling bug: Single string observations are incorrectly split into individual characters. Expected ['Alice won an award in 2023'] but got ['w', 'w', 'd', '2', '0', '2', '3'].
Search returns inconsistent results. Found Charlie and DataPlatform for 'data' query but failed to find Alice, Bob, MIT, or Python entities that exist in the graph.
Returns empty entities and relations for all valid entity names (Alice, Bob, TechCorp, ProjectAlpha, MIT, Python).

save_scenario.error: save_scenario returned empty entities {} and empty relations [], losing all previously loaded data. Expected 6 original entities + 1 created (Carol) + 1 created (DataPlatform) and 8 original relations + 1 created (Alice collaborates_with Bob).

errors.details: When passing a single string as 'contents' (e.g., 'New observation added'), the string is split into individual characters: ['N', 'e', 'w', ' ', 'o', 'b', 's', 'e', 'r', 'v', 'a', 't', 'i', 'o', 'n', ' ', 'a', 'd', 'd', 'e', 'd']
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: contents string preserved as single observation. Actual: string converted to list of individual characters
errors.root_cause: The tool wrapper accepts string for 'contents' parameter but the API implementation may be iterating over the string character-by-character instead of treating it as a single observation. The API should check if contents is a string and wrap it in a list before processing.

errors.details: Input to create_relations uses fields 'from' and 'to', but save_scenario output shows 'from_entity' and 'to_entity'
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: field names remain consistent (either 'from'/'to' OR 'from_entity'/'to_entity' throughout). Actual: create_relations accepts 'from'/'to' but save_scenario returns 'from_entity'/'to_entity'
errors.root_cause: The API internally uses different field names than the tool interface exposes. This could cause confusion for users who save and reload scenarios, as the saved format differs from the input format.

save_scenario.error: Schema inconsistency in relations: input uses 'from'/'to' fields but output uses 'from_entity'/'to_entity' fields

medium.details: When 'contents' is a single string, the tool iterates over characters instead of treating it as a single observation. Input 'Alice won an award in 2023' was split into ['w', 'w', 'd', '2', '0', '2', '3'].
medium.root_cause: The API likely expects 'contents' to be a list. When a string is passed, Python iterates over it character by character. The tool should wrap single strings in a list before calling the API.

low.details: Tool accepts relations with 'from' and 'to' keys, but the API likely expects 'from_entity' and 'to_entity' keys (as used in scenario data). Relation {from: 'Carol', to: 'TechCorp', relationType: 'works_at'} was skipped despite both entities existing.
low.root_cause: The tool wrapper uses different field names ('from'/'to') than what the underlying API expects ('from_entity'/'to_entity'), causing a mismatch when the API tries to look up entities.

low.details: After loading scenario and creating new entities, save_scenario returned empty entities and relations. All data loaded via load_scenario was lost.
low.root_cause: The load_scenario method in the API likely doesn't properly persist data to the entities/relations stores that save_scenario reads from. The API might be using separate data structures or the load operation is not integrated with the entity/relation creation methods.

low.details: search_nodes returned results for 'data' query (Charlie, DataPlatform) but not for 'MIT' or 'software' queries. open_nodes returns empty for all valid entity names. The search and retrieval logic appears broken or querying a different data store.
low.root_cause: The load_scenario likely populated a different data structure than what search_nodes and open_nodes query. The API may have inconsistent internal state or the tools are querying different collections.

errors.details: When add_observations is called with a single string in contents field, the string gets split into individual characters. For 'Alice has 5 years of experience', the result was ['h', '5', 'f', 'x', 'p'] instead of the full string.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: ['Alice has 5 years of experience'] | Actual: ['h', '5', 'f', 'x', 'p'] - individual characters from the string
errors.root_cause: The API's add_observations method appears to be iterating over the string contents instead of wrapping it in a list when a single string is passed. The tool wrapper does wrap strings in lists, but the API might be re-iterating or there's a character-level processing issue.

errors.details: After loading a clean scenario with only Alice entity, subsequent tool calls revealed pre-existing entities (Charlie, StartupXYZ) and relations (Alice-Charlie colleague_of, Charlie-TechCorp works_at, Alice-Bob collaborates_with) that were not part of the loaded scenario.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: State contains only Alice (and entities/relations we created during testing) | Actual: State contains Charlie, StartupXYZ, and pre-existing relations from unknown previous state
errors.root_cause: The MemoryKnowledgeGraphAPI instance may not be properly isolated per client_id. It appears to be a singleton or shared instance that retains data from previous test runs, violating the expected isolation for each client session.

errors.details: Alice entity shows observations that were not in the original loaded scenario: 'Duplicate Alice', 'Works remotely on Tuesdays', 'Active since 2023', plus the corrupted character-split observations.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: ['Alice is a software engineer', 'Alice works at TechCorp'] | Actual: Contains multiple unrelated observations plus corruption
errors.root_cause: This is a manifestation of the state pollution issue - the Alice entity was already modified before our test run began, suggesting the API wasn't in a clean state when load_scenario was called.

tool_execution.error: Observation string corruption - single string observations get split into individual characters instead of being stored as a single observation string
Search returns corrupted Alice observations; also returns data from previous test runs (Charlie, StartupXYZ entities and their relations) indicating state pollution
Returns corrupted Alice observations with split characters and polluted state from previous runs

save_scenario.error: Saved scenario contains pre-existing entities (Charlie, StartupXYZ) and corrupted Alice observations not in original scenario

errors.details: When passing a string value for 'contents' field, the tool splits the string into individual characters instead of treating it as a single observation. Input: 'Works remotely on Tuesdays', Output: ['W', 'o', 'r', 'k', 's', ...]
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: contents=['Works remotely on Tuesdays']. Actual: contents=['W', 'o', 'r', 'k', 's', ' ', 'r', 'e', 'm', 'o', 't', 'e', 'l', 'y', ' ', 'o', 'n', ' ', 'T', 'u', 'e', 's', 'd', 'a', 'y', 's']
errors.root_cause: The add_observations wrapper normalizes string to list but may be iterating over the string again at the API level, or there's a missing check. The code sets obs['contents'] = [contents] for string input, but somewhere the string is being treated as an iterable and split.

errors.details: Entity 'Alice' was loaded with observations ['Alice is a software engineer', 'Alice lives in New York']. When create_entities was called with 'Alice' having observation 'Duplicate Alice', the original observations were replaced instead of skipped. The saved scenario shows Alice has observations ['Duplicate Alice', ...split chars...] instead of preserving original data.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Alice should have been skipped or merged with existing. Original observations should be preserved. Actual: Alice observations were replaced entirely.
errors.root_cause: create_entities is not properly checking for existing entities before creating. The API allows entities with duplicate names to overwrite existing ones instead of being skipped.

errors.details: Original scenario loaded TechCorp as entityType='Organization' with observations ['TechCorp is a software company', 'Founded in 2010']. Search and save show TechCorp as entityType='Company' with observation 'TechCorp is a tech startup'.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: TechCorp entityType='Organization', observations=['TechCorp is a software company', 'Founded in 2010']. Actual: entityType='Company', observations=['TechCorp is a tech startup']
errors.root_cause: The scenario data loaded is not being stored correctly, or there's pre-existing data in the API that wasn't cleared, or the load_scenario implementation is not properly replacing existing data.

errors.details: Query 'software' returned empty entities array despite Alice having 'software engineer' and TechCorp having 'software company' in observations. Query 'New York' returned empty despite Alice 'lives in New York'.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: search_nodes('software') should return Alice and TechCorp entities. Actual: Empty results for both queries.
errors.root_cause: The search implementation may not be searching observation contents, or the stored data is different from what was loaded, or case sensitivity issues.

errors.details: Entity 'StartupXYZ' appears in saved scenario but was never created during the validation session.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Only entities from loaded scenario (Alice, Bob, TechCorp) plus created entity (Charlie). Actual: StartupXYZ also present.
errors.root_cause: The API instance may have pre-existing data that wasn't cleared by load_scenario, indicating load_scenario doesn't properly reset the graph state.

errors.details: Original relation loaded was 'Alice colleague_of Bob'. open_nodes and save_scenario show 'Alice collaborates_with Bob'.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: relationType='colleague_of'. Actual: relationType='collaborates_with'
errors.root_cause: Either the relation was never stored correctly, or there's data corruption during retrieval, or pre-existing data with different relation types exists.

tool_execution.error: String content incorrectly split into individual characters. Expected single observation 'Works remotely on Tuesdays' but got array of 26 single-character strings.
Search queries for 'software' and 'New York' returned empty results despite matching content in entities. Returned entity 'TechCorp' has different entityType and observations than loaded scenario.
Returned inconsistent data: Alice shows character-split observation, relation type changed from 'colleague_of' to 'collaborates_with', TechCorp has entityType 'Company' instead of 'Organization'.

save_scenario.error: Saved state does not match loaded scenario plus modifications. Original entities Alice, TechCorp have different observations/entityType. Original Bob is missing expected relations. Extra entity 'StartupXYZ' present that was not created during validation.

errors.details: When adding single string observation 'Alice received an award in 2023', the result was split into individual characters: ['A', 'i', 'c', 'c', 'i', 'v', 'w', 'i', '2', '0', '2', '3']
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Single observation string 'Alice received an award in 2023'. Actual: Array of single characters
errors.root_cause: The add_observations tool has a bug in string handling. Looking at the code, it normalizes contents but may be passing the string incorrectly to the API, causing iteration over characters instead of treating as a single observation.

errors.details: After loading scenario with entities Alice, Bob, TechCorp (Organization), ProjectAlpha, Python, MIT - the open_nodes call returns different data. Alice has corrupted observations (fragmented characters, 'Duplicate Alice', etc.), TechCorp has entityType 'Company' instead of 'Organization', and ProjectAlpha is missing from results despite being in the query.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Original scenario entities with correct observations. Actual: Corrupted/altered entity data that doesn't match the loaded scenario
errors.root_cause: The scenario loading may not be properly initializing/clearing state, or the API may have pre-existing data that wasn't cleared. The loaded scenario appears to have been merged with or overwritten by other data.

errors.details: Searching for 'Python' and 'software' returned empty entities and relations arrays, even though Python entity exists in the scenario and Alice has observations containing 'software engineer'.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Python entity and related entities returned. Actual: Empty results
errors.root_cause: The search_nodes API implementation may not be properly searching through observations, or the scenario data was not properly loaded/indexed for searching.

errors.details: Saved scenario contains only 5 entities (Charlie, Alice, StartupXYZ, Bob, TechCorp) instead of original 6+ entities. ProjectAlpha, Python, MIT are missing. Alice's observations are severely corrupted with character fragments. TechCorp has wrong entityType.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: Original scenario with 6 entities plus any newly created ones. Actual: Mixed/corrupted state with wrong entity types and fragmented observations
errors.root_cause: The API state is shared across scenarios and not properly isolated. The scenario_003 load did not create a clean state, resulting in data from previous operations being mixed in.

tool_execution.error: Observations corrupted - string split into individual characters
Search returns empty results for existing entities
Entity data corrupted and does not match loaded scenario

save_scenario.error: Saved state does not match expected scenario state - entities corrupted, missing, or modified

errors.details: When adding observations with string content, the string 'Enjoys hiking' was stored as an array of individual characters: ['E', 'n', 'j', 'o', 'y', 's', ' ', 'h', 'i', 'k', 'i', 'n', 'g'] instead of being wrapped as a single-element array ['Enjoys hiking'].
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: observations array containing ['Enjoys hiking']. Actual: observations array containing ['E', 'n', 'j', 'o', 'y', 's', ' ', 'h', 'i', 'k', 'i', 'n', 'g']
errors.root_cause: The add_observations tool wrapper appears to have a bug in how it handles string content normalization. The string 'Enjoys hiking' was iterated character-by-character instead of being wrapped in an array.

tool_execution.error: Observation string incorrectly stored as individual characters. The content 'Enjoys hiking' was stored as ['E', 'n', 'j', 'o', 'y', 's', ' ', 'h', 'i', 'k', 'i', 'n', 'g'] instead of ['Enjoys hiking'].

save_scenario.error: Saved state contains corrupted observation data - the string 'Enjoys hiking' was incorrectly stored as individual characters in Alice's observations.
